SPY $709.00 +0.48 (+0.1%)QQQ $646.24 -1.67 (-0.3%)VIX 19.27 +0.32 (+1.7%)GEX $2592.4B +2365.8B
SPY hits extreme negative gamma territory while skew compresses across major indices, signaling potential volatility regime shift.
WHAT CHANGED
SPY gamma flipped to extreme negative levels at -$1.07B (79th percentile), creating heightened sensitivity to directional moves despite modest +0.07% gain
Massive straddle activity in SPY with 187K calls and 279K puts at the 710/709/708 strikes, suggesting hedging ahead of volatility expansion
Skew compression hitting multi-asset (SPY, QQQ, NVDA all showing 2nd percentile readings) indicates options market pricing reduced tail risk despite negative gamma setup
Tech weakness emerging with QQQ lagging SPY by 33bps while VIX creeps higher to 19.27
NOTABLE DIVERGERS
IBIT and KWEB both at 96th percentile IV rank, significantly elevated vs crypto/China equity calm
EEM, XLY, and IWM all showing 83rd+ percentile IV despite relatively contained spot moves
The negative gamma combined with compressed skew creates an unusual setup where small moves could amplify quickly, yet the market isn't pricing significant tail risk.

Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 13:32 ET
Index puts surge despite single-name call buying as dispersion trade builds ahead of GDP
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Extreme P/C divergence signals institutional dispersion hedges — index puts (1.73) against single-name calls (0.64) at widest spread in months.
  • 2. Stock-specific vol regime (IC=0.04) confirms dispersion opportunity with single-name vol trading more efficiently than index protection.
  • 3. NVDA's massive 80k June call block contradicts Millennium's recent conviction builds, suggesting profit-taking into strength.
NARRATIVE — dispersion greed ahead GDP

The most striking signal today is the extreme divergence between index and single-name options flow, with institutions loading index puts (P/C 1.73) while aggressively buying single-name calls (P/C 0.64) — the widest spread since the March banking crisis. This classic dispersion hedge structure makes sense given implied correlation at just 0.04, indicating stock-specific drivers dominate over systematic risk ahead of Thursday's GDP print. NVDA's 80,000-lot June 170 call block at $33.51 stands out as the session's largest single trade, yet contradicts the bullish institutional narrative from 13F data showing Millennium's 93% Q4 addition with call overlays. The disconnect suggests early profit-taking by fast money into the chip rally, particularly with NVDA showing death cross technicals despite the broader tech strength. Meanwhile, financial sector hedging intensified with dual XLF put blocks (15k July 45P, 7k May 50P) as banks approach earnings season. Vol surface divergences amplify the dispersion theme, with 6 names showing rich vol/call-heavy patterns (WMT, CSCO, PANW) while backwardation in 8 names including ARM and COIN signals elevated near-term event risk. The combination of positive dealer gamma ($1.4B SPY GEX) and Thursday's GDP catalyst creates a compressed vol environment where single-name opportunities eclipse index trades.

Layer 1: Sentiment +55Layer 2: GEX +$1.4BLayer 3: P/C split 1.73/0.64Layer 4: IC 0.04 stock-specific
